skill.name: file-name-wizard
skill.description: Audit repository filenames and naming conventions against CLAUDE.md standards, directory patterns, and common codebase organization practices.
skill.summary: Reviews repository file names, naming standards, and directory patterns, then produces a prioritized naming audit report.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby shell backticks and weak cryptography were reviewed and dismissed as Markdown false positives. The skill has a legitimate but broad filesystem scope because it asks the assistant to enumerate repository files, read naming guidance, inspect file contents, and write a Markdown audit report.

medium_findings.title: Broad Repository File Access for Naming Audit
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to find all repository files, read CLAUDE.md files, inspect file contents, and create an audit report. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but users should expect broad filesystem reads and a local report write.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require file discovery, reading project guidance, checking file contents, and writing a report. This confirms filesystem access, but no malicious network, credential, or command execution behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detection from Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code and fenced report examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. No evidence found of command execution instructions or dynamic shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown examples that quote filenames, suffixes, directory names, and report templates. The file contains no Ruby code and no backtick shell execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection from Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in prose and Markdown naming references, not cryptographic API usage. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption routines, hashing code, or password handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of each flagged line shows natural language about descriptions, contents, or Markdown files. There is no cryptographic operation to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent file names make codebases harder to scan and maintain. This skill audits repository naming patterns against CLAUDE.md standards and produces a prioritized report.
actual_capabilities: Finds source, configuration, documentation, and test files for naming review.
Extracts naming rules from root and directory CLAUDE.md files.
Compares filenames with explicit standards and local directory patterns.
Checks file contents to confirm names match their purpose.
Groups violations by severity, directory, issue type, and file type.
Writes a Markdown audit report with suggested names and priorities.
limitations: It does not rename files or update imports.
It depends on accessible repository files and accurate CLAUDE.md guidance.
It may need human review before naming changes are applied.
It audits naming conventions, not runtime behavior or code quality.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Filenames
use_cases.description: Review component directories for mixed casing, redundant suffixes, and names that do not match local conventions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Repository Cleanup Plan
use_cases.description: Find temporary, generic, dated, or misleading filenames before a refactor or release stabilization effort.

use_cases.title: Document Missing Naming Rules
use_cases.description: Identify directories without clear naming standards and produce recommendations for CLAUDE.md documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Naming Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this repository for filename consistency. Compare files with existing CLAUDE.md rules and summarize the most important issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you want a quick first pass across the repository.

prompt_templates.title: Directory-Focused Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit filenames under the selected directory. Identify the local naming pattern, list outliers, and suggest better names.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a specific area has inconsistent naming.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Refactor Naming Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the repository for temporary, dated, generic, misleading, and versioned filenames. Group findings by severity and rename effort.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before cleanup work or a large rename plan.

prompt_templates.title: Standards Gap Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit filename conventions and identify directories that lack clear standards. Recommend CLAUDE.md naming guidance for each gap.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when teams need stronger naming documentation.

output_examples.input: Audit filenames in a TypeScript service repository.
output_examples.output: A Markdown report that lists inconsistent service suffixes, generic helper names, wrong casing, and suggested replacements by severity.

output_examples.input: Check component filenames against local conventions.
output_examples.output: A summary of component naming patterns, outliers, redundant suffixes, and files whose names do not match their contents.

output_examples.input: Find missing naming standards for project directories.
output_examples.output: A documentation gap report that names directories without rules and recommends concise CLAUDE.md standards.

best_practices: Run the audit before large refactors so rename work can be planned safely.
Review suggested names with maintainers before applying file renames.
Keep CLAUDE.md naming rules current so future audits have clear standards.
anti_patterns: Do not use the report as automatic approval to rename files without checking imports.
Do not ignore local directory patterns when global standards are incomplete.
Do not treat generic names as harmless when they hide important ownership boundaries.

faq.question: Does this skill rename files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for audit and reporting only.

faq.question: What standards does it use?
faq.answer: It checks CLAUDE.md guidance, local directory patterns, and common file naming practices.

faq.question: Can it audit large repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but large repositories may take longer because every relevant file is reviewed.

faq.question: Does it read file contents?
faq.answer: Yes. It may inspect file contents to confirm whether names match file purpose.

faq.question: What output does it create?
faq.answer: It creates a Markdown audit report with issue counts, priorities, affected directories, and suggested names.

faq.question: Is it safe for private codebases?
faq.answer: It runs locally in the assistant context, but users should expect broad repository reads during the audit.
